chapter_title.en: Chapter on Endowments, Charity, and Gifts

parts.text: 5571 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى اَلْعُبَيْدِيُّ قَالَ

text: كَتَبَ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَمْزَةَ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : مُدَبَّرٌ وُقِفَ ثُمَّ مَاتَ صَاحِبُهُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ لاَ يَفِي بِمَالِهِ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "يُبَاعُ وَقْفُهُ فِي اَلدَّيْنِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5571 - Muhammad ibn Isa Al-Ubaydi narrated that Ahmad ibn Hamzah wrote to Abu Al-Hasan, peace be upon him, regarding a mudabbar (a slave who was set to be freed upon his owner's death) who was placed in an endowment (waqf), and then his owner died while owing a debt that could not be covered by his remaining wealth.
Imam (as) wrote in response:
"The endowment is to be sold to settle the debt."
